resultData.title: The Desktop Adventures of SGI Indy

game.title: The Desktop Adventures of SGI Indy
game.short_text: Slay the Spire inspired rogue like deck builder with retro operating system themes.

details.title: The Desktop Adventures of SGI Indy
details.description:
The Desktop Adventures of SGI Indy
WHERE DID THINGS GO WRONG?
Late one night, down an abandoned hallway at CERN, the passing text of someone playing the INFOCOM  Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy coalesces into the memory of an idle SGI Indy workstation. Imbued with sentience and a need to understand, SGI Indy sets out to explore the strange places that computing history left behind.
The Desktop Adventures of SGI Indy is a roguelike deckbuilder set inside the forgotten operating systems of the 1990s.
----
How to Play
Build a deck of Unix shell commands. Fight rogue processes. Explore the map.
Cards are shell commands:  `ls`, `kill`, `fork`, `grep`, `dd` -- each with upgrade pathsRelics are vintage hardware peripherals with passive abilitiesRest sites let you heal, purge cards, or upgradeEvents are encounters with the remnants of these systemsEnemies are the rogue processes running inside them
----
The Strange Places
IRIX: Silicon Graphics. Jurassic Park ran on thisBeOS: Multithreaded, media-first.NeXTSTEP: Where the web was invented.QNX:  Real-time. Running inside systems you've never thought about.
----
Made for Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1 - Theme: Strange Places

----
Post-launch updates:
- Hopefully the sounds are not so ear-shattering (applied a -7.0db nerf to the sounds bus)- Tweaked the boss fight, persistent display of boss status effects. Reduced scaling.

game.short_text: Narrative game about building your life once choice at a time
game.title: Nawia

details.title: Nawia
details.description:
Web version should run fine but it takes a minute to load in, just let it. 
Nawia is a game about building your life once choice at a time. It's a narrative driven game made for Brackeys 2026.1 Game Jam.
This game is inspired by real events.All characters and events depicted in this game are fictional.

Credit where it's due
3D Assets 
Most of the assets come from Pizza Doggy's various collections.
Besides that:Doggo by mcsteegModified typewriterPaper textureSkyboxes by Screaming Brain StudiosCar model by ggbot
Music
Made by genius Clavier-Music 
SFX
From Soundly collection



Spoiler Section - Click to reveal, check out after playing the game!
Nav (Polish: Nawia, "NAH-vyah") is the Slavic realm of the dead, a spiritual world where the souls of ancestors dwell after leaving the living world. In this case visualised as a series of rooms representing one's life, this is where the game takes place. The symbol that you can see a few times in the game is a modern symbol of god Veles, who exercises custody over Nawia.There's no other endings for you to explore. Some of the most important choices in our lives are not ours to be made. Appreciate what you can choose.This game's description lies a little. It's obviously not allowing you to build your life as you want. It's set in 2012-present Ukraine, following just one of many people that was struck by tragedies during this time.DisclaimerI am Polish, not Ukrainian. I didn't live through any of this but know a few people that did. This game is not trying to make a political statement. War causes suffering to ordinary people all over the world, this is just a little closer to home for me.Trivia- Each room contains a calendar that helps you understand the timeline- You probably noticed some floating objects, they are actually present in all rooms foreshadowing that this might not be just a normal world. They just get less and less subtle with each room.- Composer of the music is actually Ukrainian. The song you hear in the bunker is actually titled Morning of February 24, probably coming from their own experience of the first day of the invasion in 2022.- Choices influence some later text, but it's rather tame, the ending is still the same. Probably the biggest change (actual change in the world) is the colour of the dog!

game.short_text: A minimalist physics puzzle game where your physical keyboard becomes a 1:1 map of the screen.
game.title: Keyscape

details.title: Keyscape
details.description:
Controls
The keyboard is your map. Where you press physically determines where gravity spawns digitally.

Letters, Numbers, , and . : Press and hold to spawn a Gravity Well at that exact spatial location on the screen. The anomaly will be pulled toward it.
Spacebar : Invert Momentum. Instantly reverses your current trajectory. (Warning: 2-second cooldown).
Hold Two Keys (for 1 second) : Rip open a Wormhole between those two spatial coordinates. Entering one port instantly teleports you to the other while preserving your entry speed.
ESC : Pause system / Change keyboard map (Supports QWERTY, QWERTZ, and AZERTY) / Adjust Audio.

Keyscape is a minimalist, physics-based puzzle game that demands high cognitive and physical coordination. You do not directly control the avatar. Instead, you control the fabric of space itself.

Your physical keyboard acts as a 1:1 spatial map of the screen. Pressing a key in the top-left of your keyboard (like 'Q') spawns a gravitational anomaly in the top-left of your screen. Pressing a key in the bottom-right (like '.') spawns one in the bottom-right. By pressing and holding different combinations of keys, you must bend gravity, manipulate momentum, and guide a vulnerable anomaly through treacherous geometric voids to the sync zone.

What starts as simple trajectory management quickly evolves into a complex dance of orbital mechanics and spatial awareness.

You are an user of the interface. And also a cat.

A puzzle-platformer game in which You control the cat and cursor to unlock the Exit button and Exit the Game. You will be required to use both keyboard and mouse (and the other mouse) to beat it. 
Good luck!

LMB - use sliders
WSAD / Arrows / Space - move and jump

The goal  of the game is to Exit the Game by unlocking the Exit Button. You have to collect green letters across different menus to do so. Use the cat to collect letters, use Your cursor to use sliders, and use in-game cursor to press buttons.
This is not the first time I've added my cat to the game. And probably not the last.

game.title: Hypersphere Aurora
game.short_text: Push back the fog and solve the mystery of the <HYPERSPHERE AURORA> in this narrative tile-placing puzzle.

details.title: Hypersphere Aurora
details.description:
Welcome to the Drift. A research station sitting in the cracks between realities has gone dark, 2 of its crew are missing. You have been tasked with remotely accessing the Drift and uncovering the mystery of the <HYPERSPHERE AURORA>Fullscreen Recommended! Walkthrough included below!Controls:
Click to interact. Select hexes adjacent to your character to move to or examine them.Select items from the "Imagine" list to form them in the fog.If you ever get trapped, you can press R to return to your starting positionESC to pause (you can turn down the sounds)
In Hypersphere Aurora, you unlock new tiles ("thoughts") by either:
Placing enough of an already unlocked tile on the mapPlacing some amount of tiles around a particular tile, then inspecting it.
You learn how to unlock most tiles by examining tiles you already have or by listening to recordings.Since this project came together in a fever pitch over a weekend, I'm sure I've misplaced a clue or made something less clear than it should be. If you're ever really stuck, I have included a walkthrough below. 

Walkthrough

This walkthrough is loosely arranged in an “expected order of unlocks”, but there are multiple branching paths.
Each entry includes a hint and the full solution.

*Even if you have the correct arrangement, you must examine the correct tile.


Unlock
Hint
Solution


Mountain

Hint
Follow the in-game hint “Imagine Nothing” for a while.



Solution
Interact with fog until there are 10 Nullspaces (empty tiles). This usually takes 3–4 triggers.




Water

Hint
Examine a Mountain. “It looks lonely.”



Solution
Imagine a Mountain next to another Mountain.




Recording – Nature

Hint
You just unlocked something new. Try imagining it.



Solution
After placing Water, step into it to unlock the recording.




Tree

Hint
Listen to Recording – Nature.



Solution
Place Water next to a Mountain.




Cabin

Hint
Examine a Tree. Follow the hint it provides; then examine the Tree again.



Solution
Place a Tree next to both a Mountain and Water.




Recording – Focus

Hint
A cabin has appeared nearby



Solution
Examine the Cabin. It describes a recipe for Snow but you don't know Hexagon yet (and might not know Field).



Field

Hint
Remember how Mountain was unlocked?



Solution
Have at least 35 Fog tiles on the map.




Flowers

Hint
A lonely Field tile isn't very “expansive”.



Solution
Step into a Field with at least three other Fields nearby.




Recording – Beauty

Hint
You recently unlocked a tile. Try placing it.



Solution
Step onto a Flower tile.




Recording – Abstraction

Hint
What does Recording – Beauty say HC is doing?



Solution
Place three Trees on the map.




Triangle

Hint
You unlocked a new recording.



Solution
Listen to Recording – Abstraction.




Hexagon

Hint
A Triangle mentions more triangles.



Solution
Examine a Triangle with two other Triangles beside it.




Cube

Hint
Examine a Triangle. It mentions something about a Mountain?.



Solution
Examine a Triangle next to a Mountain.




Recording – Strangeness

Hint
You just unlocked Cube.



Solution
Examine a Cube.




Recording – Control

Hint
Do you feel compelled to place more Hexagons?



Solution
Have six Hexagons on the map.




Void

Hint
You just found Recording – Control.



Solution
Examine Recording – Control.




Sphere

Hint
Examining Cube tells you to bring all your shapes together. What shapes do you have?



Solution
Examine a Cube next to a Triangle and Hexagon.




Hypersphere

Hint
One V01D might not be strong enough.



Solution
Examine Sphere with at least two Voids beside it.




Stars

Hint
Recording – Strangeness II mentions HC doing something unusual.



Solution
Examine a Void fully surrounded by Nullspace.




Snow

Hint
The recipe appeared earlier in a recording.



Solution
Examine a Hexagon next to a Mountain and Field.




Tundra

Hint
Ada and Elias tried making lots of snow.



Solution
Imagine at least six Snow tiles.




Aurora

Hint
The Tundra tile mentions the sky is black. What might appear in a dark sky?



Solution
Step onto Tundra next to two Stars.




Event: Hypersphere Aurora

Hint
It’s the name of the game. HC says he will do something to the Aurora.



Solution
Examine a Hypersphere next to an Aurora (or vice versa).




Recording – Reach/Grasp
-

Solution
Appears after the Hypersphere Aurora event.




Computer
-

Solution
Appears after listening to Recording – Reach/Grasp.




Home
-

Solution
Appears after examining the Computer.

game.short_text: Perform medium service with your typewriter
game.title: Last words of the late Arthur Jerymn

details.title: Last words of the late Arthur Jerymn
details.description:
Last words of the late Arthur Jerymn
Created in 7 days for Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1. Theme: Strange Places
Ever wonder how formal a seance could be? Why not type it down and make it on the record!  

Important Hints
Some explanation of the correction mechanics. Correction can be done via
Backspace (using the backspace key) to the position you want to clear out
Put in the correction stripe (using the tab key). This should insert the stripe in front of the type head
Type the same wrong letters you wish to clear

So, for example, if you typed "A quick brown dog..." and want to change "dog" to 'fox", you will need to backspace the caret to the "d" in "dog", put in the correction stripe, and type "dog" once again. The "dog" should be now cleared, and you can type fox in the correct position again. 

You can zoom in while at the typewriter view as well. Doing so will show what letters are currently visible and may require correction.
If you cannot type [:] try using the key next to the [L] Key.
Synopsis
You play as a professional medium service provider in the late Victorian era. Being hired by the wife of the recently deceased Arthur Jerymn, you are tasked with retrieving the missing will through your spiritual power. But beware, speaking to the dead is a dangerous endeavour, and can easily take one's life if not careful. Can you complete your job safely and uncover the hidden secrets of Arthur Jerymn?

Gameplay
The game is a typing game with a horror twist. The goal of the game is to correctly type out the true words of the dead while ignoring all decoys. An arrow ornament will point towards the correct words as guidance. The game features a simulated typewriter machine, with the correction mechanics being the highlight of the simulation. The game will get progressively harder with other anomalies such as possessed typing and key swapping, and challenge the typing skill of the player. Incorrectly typing or taking too long to do so would result in the player losing the game.
The game also contains a short story that slowly unravels itself during the gameplay and would conclude neatly at the true ending of the game.

Controls
[Left Ctrl], [Right Ctrl]: Toggle between typewriter and room[Left Mouse Button]: Zoom in both typewriter and room view[Tab]: Toggles between correction stripe and none.[Keyboard Keys]: Typing

Theme interpretation
"Strange Places" as "strange" and "places" literally, where you are in a strange and uncanny room when performing the seance, the words are in strange places in the room, and the key might swap to different places on the typewriter".


Team
Kelvin - Design, Programmer, 3D Model, Artworkfanngaipui - Design, Audio, Playtest

Version History

game.short_text: Short exploration game with liminal vibes: wander and don't question the hotel
game.title: Overlook Inn

details.title: Overlook Inn
details.description:
Press the P button to change the mouse sensitivity.
Controls

Movement: WASD / Left Stick.
Camera: Mouse / Right Stick.
Interact: Left Click / A.
Flashlight: Shift / Trigger.
Pause: P / Start.

Welcome to a perfectly respectable hotel. Nothing strange is happening here.
After a sudden loud noise startled an elderly guest, her cats bolted and are now hiding somewhere in the building. As the hotel receptionist (and the only one willing to deal with this situation), it’s your job to track them down and return them safely.
 
Search through an old hotel that feels just slightly… off. Corridors don’t always behave the way they should. Rooms seem to appear where they weren’t before. Something about the place is unsettling — but the task at hand remains the same: find those cats.
 
A short exploration game blending serie, liminal-space vibes with a lighthearted, silly tone. Wander, listen for meows, and try not to question the hotel too much.
 
Features
 
  Explore a strange, ever-so-slightly wrong hotel. Hunt down runaway cats in unexpected places. Unsettling atmosphere mixed with absurdist humor. Small secrets and odd events scattered throughout. Made for Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1, theme: Strange Places.

details.title: Catato?!
details.description:
Catato?! is our entry for the Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1 with the theme Strange Places.
This is our take on a unique farming sim where the goal is to create crop hybrids by planting different crops next to existing ones. If you solve the planting mini-games correctly, the chances of generating new crops are higher.
 
To fit the theme of the jam, these new mutations get weirder and weirder... If you generate enough weird mutations, you might just get transported to a new world...
 
We hope you enjoy Catato?!. We had a lot of fun creating this entry but, as it goes in these events, plenty of our ideas are missing from the final build and there's some bugs that we didn't have time to fix.
All of the assets were created for this jam, except for a few sound effects taken from Pixabay. No AI used.
All programming (and some sfx) was done by jpiolho.
 
All music was composed by Saca.
 
All art assets, sprites, and UI were drawn by SaraFilipa.
 
Game design and testing were a collaborative effort.
 
Thank you for playing!

game.short_text: Old-school dungeon crawling drenched in blood and shadows.
game.title: Cursed Blight

details.title: Cursed Blight
details.description:
Cursed Blight is a short, single-player, old-school dungeon-crawling adventure steeped in unforgiving darkness. 

Atmospheric 3D dungeon crawler gameplay with 2D sprites;Simple but deadly combat, inspired by MÖRK BORG's doom-soaked brutality;Roaming enemies to avoid or fight;Flesh scattered across the map and dropped by enemies, use it to heal, but make sure to have all that you can carry by the end;Chests that upgrade your equipment;Hidden levers that unlock doors for progression, shortcuts, upgrades, or flesh.

You come from a village slowly rotting beneath an unending plague.
The disease is both a curse and a calling. Those who bear its marks are declared chosen, named Blessed by the elders. They speak of it with dry mouths and lowered eyes: a sacred pilgrimage beneath the earth.
Today, the signs bloom upon your skin.
Explore the dark labyrinthine catacombs, scavenge equipment left behind by previous Blessed, harvest the flesh of fallen foes old and new, and seek the Holy Grail to cleanse your village of the spreading Cursed Blight.
But beware, as human-like cultists stalk the depths, hunting for fresh sacrifices to feed their master.

game.title: Odd Earworms
game.short_text: Rythm Game with interactive Backgrounds

details.title: Odd Earworms
details.description:
Bring the beat to the bizarre! Created for the Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1 under the theme "Strange Places", Odd Earworms is a rhythm game where your musical timing breathes life into the unknown. Strange, desolate places across the world need your help, and the only cure is a good melody! Take control of a colorful quartet of musical worms. As you hit the right notes, not only does your score go up, but the strange places around you will actively react and come alive to your performance. Features: Music customly made for the Jam: by Merulon Dynamic Environments: Watch the background react in real-time to your performance. Keep your combo up to make a difference! Rhythmic Variety: Tap the dots precisely on the beat, and hold down the keys during long streaks to rack up massive multipliers. Meet the Band: Command four expressive, colorful worms, each dedicated to their own lane. You can play using either the keyboard's Arrow Keys or W / A / S / D. Left Lane (Purple Worm): Left Arrow or A Down Lane (Blue Worm): Down Arrow or S Up Lane (Green Worm): Up Arrow or W Right Lane (Red Worm): Right Arrow or D

How to Play: Watch the notes fall from the top of the screen. Press the corresponding key when a dot reaches the hit zone. Hold the key down for continuous streaks to maximize your score. Don't miss, or the environment might lose its groove! Submission for the Brackeys Jam 2026.1 - Theme: Strange Places.

game.short_text: Grow a strange alien world, shape its life grid by grid, and uncover the memories hidden beneath the stars.
game.title: Astral Bloom

details.title: Astral Bloom
details.description:
I am actively working on the game! Please give me your ideas and feedback, and I will add them :)
 

Astral Bloom is a relaxing sci-fi incremental builder where you cultivate a mysterious alien planet. Gather exotic resources, grow strange plants, and automate your world with loyal companions. Watch as the void slowly comes alive around you.
Expand your planet, unlock new lifeforms, upgrades, and uncover fragments of a forgotten past through gentle storytelling and meaningful choices. At the end, will you stay on the world you've grown, or leave in search of something lost among the stars?

Master the ability to control day or night, boosting different benefits.

Instructions:
WASD/Arrow keys to moveBuild things at the bottom (shortcut keys F1~F5 only available on Downloadable versions)Upgrades on the leftResources on the rightC to toggle ability (after 2 Wishes)ESC to cancel
Notes:
If the game is too big or small, use Ctrl + Mouse wheel to zoom the webpage.
WebGL 2.0 required for web. For best experience, play in Chrome, Firefox or Edge, or the downloadable executable.
Roadmap
I'm hard at work behind the scenes on new features. A full devlog on the roadmap will be posted but meantime, here's a sneak peek.
Planet Mutators - select a random modifier each runNew buildings - Campfires, FountainsWorld buildings - Monoliths, pay a tribute for a new unlockCharacter Customization - Unlock accessories, skins, and expressionsMeta Upgrades - Either a research tree or node unlocks.Sanctuary Mode - Play idly without timers to admire your creation
Got any ideas? Let me know and I'll add them!



Steam Release!
Steam demo goes live on 13th March!

This kinda tracks with how my past three games were all born from jams.

game.short_text: A short experimental horror experience.
game.title: I FEEL GREEN

details.title: I FEEL GREEN
details.description:
I FEEL GREEN
About the Game
I FEEL GREEN is a short experimental horror experience about discovering cursed data and overwriting reality. 
This project focuses on atmosphere and psychological unease rather than traditional mechanics. The goal was to create something unsettling, immersive, and driven by mood instead of gameplay systems. With this release, I intentionally stepped outside my comfort zone to experiment with tone and presentation.
 
Estimated Playtime
Approximately 5–6 minutes.
 
Recommended Experience
For the best experience:
 
 🎧 Play with headphones
 
 🌑 Turn the lights off
 
 🖥️ Download the game for better performance
 

Performance Notes
 Some browsers may experience performance issues.
 
 Microsoft Edge currently provides the most stable web experience.
 
 A system that supports WebGL2 is required to run the browser version.
 

If you encounter performance problems, downloading the standalone file is strongly recommended.

game.short_text: a short contemplative adventure on an alien moon
game.title: Mycelara

details.title: Mycelara
details.description:
Explore a strange alien world and take the best photos. Discover surreal creatures and incredible landscapes!
This is a short game made in one week during the Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1.
Gameplay:
Move with WASD & mouse or with a gamepad.Take photos with left click or the left gamepad button.Explore the world!If you are stuck, try taking photos from a very short distance. It may trigger some events (also, the game may be a bit glitchy at times, so you might have to try several times).At one point, the path is blocked, and you’ll have to find another way up. Look for something you can climb.
Credits:
Olivier Guillaume:programmer, animator, level designer, 3D modeling
Henning Behrendt:programmer, sound designer, composerAlex Azorin Sanchis:3D artist

Assets used:
Fantasy skybox FREE (Render knight)Handpainted Grass & Ground Textures (Chromisu)SFXs: Gregor Quendel Collection, Symphobia 3: Lumina

game.short_text: Forge your own routes, meet allies, upgrade your caravan, and set out to find new, strange places.
game.title: Caravan Trader

details.title: Caravan Trader
details.description:
Play as a trader across strange lands. Battle along routes and trade with merchants to become the richest trader!

How to Play
1. Travel to a town and buy items at a discounted price
2. Travel to another town to sell those items at a higher price.
3. Upgrade your caravan for more dangerous expeditions!
